1. Document Control
   Purpose: To establish the formal control framework for the document.
   Expected content: Title, versioning, approval authority, and revision history.
   Sample elements:
   1.1 Document Title: Metadata Principles Document
   1.2 Version and Status: Version 1.0, Draft
   1.3 Approval Authority: Data Governance Council
   1.4 Revision History: Date, Description of Changes, Author

2. Executive Summary
   Purpose: To provide a high-level overview of the document's intent and significance.
   Expected content: Summary of the document's purpose, key objectives, and expected outcomes.
   Sample elements: This document outlines the principles for metadata management, establishing governance boundaries and roles to ensure effective oversight and stewardship of metadata across the organization.

3. Domain and Context Framing
   Purpose: To define the specific domain and contextual factors influencing the governance framework.
   Expected content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and maturity positioning based on the maturity score.
   Sample elements:
   3.1 Domain Definition: Metadata Management
   3.2 Organizational Context: Medium-sized enterprise with diverse data assets
   3.3 Maturity Positioning: Score 0 - Non-existent governance structure

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly articulate the action being defined and its parameters.
   Expected content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.
   Sample elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: MD-01 - Define metadata management scope, principles, and governance boundaries
   4.2 Action Objective: Establish a foundational understanding of metadata management principles.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Focus on business, technical, and operational metadata types.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.
   Expected content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and a detailed step-by-step execution structure.
   Sample elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To define enterprise metadata principles effectively.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires a data governance foundation.
   5.3 Deliverables: Metadata principles document.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Conduct Stakeholder Workshop
   - Identify Metadata Types
   - Define Metadata Principles
   - Clarify Roles and Responsibilities
   - Document Principles Using DAMA Structure
   - Review and Validate Principles Document
   - Obtain Approval
   - Communicate and Implement
   - Establish Governance Mechanisms

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To delineate the structural components of governance relevant to the action.
   Expected content: Coverage areas, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, governance processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: Data Governance Office, Data Governance Council
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All organizational data assets
   6.3 Governance Themes: Metadata management, data stewardship
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Hierarchical representation of governance roles
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles for Data Owner, Data Steward, and Data Custodian
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Stakeholder engagement
   - Metadata categorization
   - Principle drafting
   - Role definition
   - Document structuring
   - Review and approval
   - Communication and training
   - Governance mechanism establishment
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Integration with data quality and data architecture governance
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Exclusion of non-metadata related data governance activities

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   Expected Content: Basic governance oversight definition aligned with foundational maturity.
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To outline the governance of the document itself, ensuring its relevance and currency.
   Expected content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.
   Sample elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document to be reviewed annually
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by Data Governance Office
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Steward responsible for document maintenance

9. Appendices
   Purpose: To provide supplementary materials that support the main document.
   Expected content: Templates, reference standards, and supporting artifacts.
   Sample elements:
   9.1 Templates: Metadata principles template
   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK
   9.3 Supporting Artefacts: Workshop notes, categorized metadata list

   Expected Content: Definition of the domain, organizational context, and current maturity positioning.

   3.2 Organizational Context: This document applies to all data assets managed within the organization.

4. Action Definition
   Purpose: To clearly define the action being addressed by the document.
   Expected Content: Action code, title, objective, and scope boundaries.
   Sample Elements:
   4.1 Action Code and Title: MD-01 - Define metadata management scope, principles, and governance boundaries
   4.2 Action Objective: To establish a comprehensive metadata management framework.
   4.3 Action Scope Boundaries: Includes technical, business, process, lineage, and quality metadata; excludes unmanaged personal notes and informal spreadsheets.

5. Procedure Definition
   Purpose: To outline the procedural steps necessary to achieve the action objective.
   Expected Content: Objective, prerequisites, deliverables, and detailed execution steps.
   Sample Elements:
   5.1 Procedure Objective: To define the metadata management scope effectively.
   5.2 Prerequisites: Requires defined principles.
   5.3 Deliverables: Metadata scope document.
   5.4 Step-by-Step Execution Structure:
   - Identify Metadata Categories
   - Link Metadata to Systems and Pipelines
   - Clarify Exclusions
   - Capture Scope Details
   - Apply CMMI Scoping Logic
   - Review and Approve Scope Document
   - Establish Governance Mechanisms
   - Finalize and Distribute Scope Document

6. Governance Structural Components
   Purpose: To define the governance framework that supports the metadata management initiative.
   Expected Content: Coverage, themes, organizational structure, decision authority, governance processes, interfaces, and exclusions.
   Sample Elements:
   6.1 Organizational Coverage: All departments utilizing data assets.
   6.2 Data Asset Coverage: All governed systems and data pipelines.
   6.3 Governance Themes: Data quality, compliance, and stewardship.
   6.4 Governance Organizational Structure: Data Governance Council, Data Governance Office, Data Owners, Data Stewards, Data Custodians.
   6.5 Decision Authority Model: Defined roles and responsibilities for decision-making.
   6.6 Governance Processes:
   - Establish metadata categories
   - Map metadata to systems
   - Document exclusions
   - Review governance framework
   - Monitor compliance
   - Engage stakeholders
   - Facilitate training
   - Conduct audits
   6.7 Interfaces with Other Governance Structures: Coordination with Data Quality and Data Architecture governance.
   6.8 Explicit Exclusions: Unmanaged personal notes and informal spreadsheets.

7. Control and Oversight Mechanisms
   Purpose: To define the formal control and oversight mechanisms applicable to this action.
   Expected Content: Basic governance oversight definition aligned with foundational maturity.
8. Document Governance
   Purpose: To define the management and review processes for the document itself.
   Expected Content: Lifecycle management, review cycle, and ownership.
   Sample Elements:
   8.1 Lifecycle Management: Document will be reviewed annually.
   8.2 Review Cycle: Annual review by the Data Governance Council.
   8.3 Ownership of the Document: Data Governance Office.

   9.2 Reference Standards: DAMA-DMBOK guidelines.
